models.searchResult: A result from the universal search, grouped by type (passage, character, theme, strongs).
models.dailyBread: Today's Daily Bread devotional state — preview for uncompleted, acknowledgment for completed.
models.historyItem: A recent study session entry — passage, character, theme, or sermon project the user visited.
models.recentNote: A recent journal entry shown on the Home screen for continuity of reflection.
models.welcomeMessage: The first-launch greeting shown when there's no history.

relationships: SearchResults are grouped by type and link to Reader, Characters, Themes, or Word Study
DailyBread links to the Daily Bread section
HistoryItems link back to the entity they reference (Reader, Character profile, Theme profile, Study Builder)
RecentNotes link to the Journal section

passages.title: The woman at the well
passages.preview: A Samaritan woman came to draw water...

passages.title: Nicodemus visits Jesus
passages.preview: Now there was a Pharisee named Nicodemus...

characters.name: Samaritan woman
characters.bioBrief: Unnamed woman at Jacob's well whose midday errand became the most important conversation of her life

themes.name: Living water
themes.modernFraming: She hears plumbing. He means something else entirely.

strongs.brief: Water, often used metaphorically for spiritual life

dailyBread.title: When someone else gets what you wanted

dailyBreadCompleted.title: When someone else gets what you wanted
dailyBreadCompleted.completedMessage: Today's bread was broken. Esau's grief is real — and so is the grace that follows it.

recentNotes.snippet: Why does the text say Jesus 'had to' pass through Samaria? Was it geographical necessity or theological intention?
She came at noon because she didn't want to be seen. I've done that — chosen the wrong time just to avoid people.

welcomeMessage: Welcome to Selah. Start anywhere — search for a passage, explore a character, or begin with today's reading below. There's no right way to use this. Follow what catches your attention. The text has been waiting for you.
